sections.paragraph:
and פִּילֶגֶשׁ with suff. פִּילַגְשֹׁו, pl. פִּלַּגְשִׁים and פִּילַגְשִׁים.

(1) f. a concubine (Ch. פִּילַקְתָּא, פְּלַקְתָּא id. With this accord πάλλαξ, παλλκίς, παλλακή; Lat. pellex. The etymology is obscure, but the origin may be sought with some appearance of truth in the idea of softness and pleasure; with the Phœnicio-Shemitic roots פלג, פלק, compare מָלַח No. I, and the observations there made; and with the Gr. παλλακίς, comp. μαλακός), Gen 22:24 35:22 Jud. 19:9 , seqq., and frequently; more fully אִשָּׁה פִילֶגֶשׁ Jud. 19:1 2Sa 15:16 20:3 .

(2) ὁ πάλλαξ, a paramour, i.q. מְאַהֵב Eze 23:20, compare verse Eze 23:5.
